channel.title: Observer Patch Holography Updates
channel.description: Public OPH links, with OPH Textbooks, Reverse Engineering Reality, and Applications as the best starting points, plus physics unification, coherence, stress-test prompts, extra demos, source notebook, essays, lab, and challenge updates.

item.title: Physics Unification via OPH
item.description: The direct public entry page for OPH as a physics-unification framework connecting gravity, quantum theory, particles, records, observers, and simulation theory.

item.title: Floating Pragma Blog
item.description: Public OPH essays and entry points connecting observer consistency to meaning, computation, semiotics, and broader physics interpretation.

item.title: OPH Applications
item.description: The OPH applications page for hardware, compute, energy, AGI, lift, and optical chamber consensus.

item.title: OPH Coherence Map
item.description: The public graph surface for OPH concepts, overlaps, and cross-domain routes.

item.title: Three-Body Problem OPH Demo
item.description: An extra simulator and proof walk-through for the OPH three-body problem solution, framed as a loop holonomy gluing example.

item.title: OPH Textbooks
item.description: The guided study surface and the best structured route into the OPH derivations.

item.title: Reverse Engineering Reality
item.description: The book-length narrative route into Observer Patch Holography and the full research framework.

item.title: Stress-Test Observer Patch Holography
item.description: Use the OPH NotebookLM source notebook, public essays, OPH Lab, papers, repository, and public challenge to find the strongest failure mode. Detailed objections should go to tom@floatingpragma.io.

item.title: OPH License and Anti-Patent Covenant
item.description: The canonical repository license and OPH open-use anti-patent covenant, with PATENTS.md carrying copy/paste website notices.

item.title: OPH NotebookLM Source Notebook
item.description: Ask the source notebook for the strongest objection, the proof chain behind a claim, or the empirical pressure point most likely to break OPH.

item.title: Semiotics and the Physics of Meaning
item.description: The bridge from signs, interpretation, shared codes, and meaning to observer overlap and stable public records.

item.title: P = NP on the Observer Screen
item.description: An essay on verification, mismatch detection, local repair, proof-of-work, and stable records as OPH computational primitives. It is not a classical P = NP proof claim.

item.title: The Trigger
item.description: A public entry point into the OPH claim and its broader stakes.
